name: twingate
description: A Pulumi package for creating and managing Twingate cloud resources.
attribution: This Pulumi package is based on the [`twingate` Terraform Provider](https://github.com/Twingate/terraform-provider-twingate).

nodejs.packageDescription: A Pulumi package for creating and managing Twingate cloud resources.
nodejs.readme:
> This provider is a derived work of the [Terraform Provider](https://github.com/Twingate/terraform-provider-twingate)
> distributed under [MPL 2.0](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/MPL/2.0/). If you encounter a bug or missing feature,
> first check the [`pulumi-twingate` repo](https://github.com/Twingate/pulumi-twingate/issues); however, if that doesn't turn up anything,
> please consult the source [`terraform-provider-twingate` repo](https://github.com/Twingate/terraform-provider-twingate/issues).

python.readme:
> This provider is a derived work of the [Terraform Provider](https://github.com/Twingate/terraform-provider-twingate)
> distributed under [MPL 2.0](https://www.mozilla.org/en-US/MPL/2.0/). If you encounter a bug or missing feature,
> first check the [`pulumi-twingate` repo](https://github.com/Twingate/pulumi-twingate/issues); however, if that doesn't turn up anything,
> please consult the source [`terraform-provider-twingate` repo](https://github.com/Twingate/terraform-provider-twingate/issues).

apiToken.description:
The access key for API operations. You can retrieve this
from the Twingate Admin Console ([documentation](https://docs.twingate.com/docs/api-overview)).
Alternatively, this can be specified using the TWINGATE_API_TOKEN environment variable.

cache.description: Specifies the cache settings for the provider.

defaultTags.description: A default set of tags applied globally to all resources created by the provider.

httpMaxRetry.description:
Specifies a retry limit for the http requests made. The default value is 10.
Alternatively, this can be specified using the TWINGATE_HTTP_MAX_RETRY environment variable

httpTimeout.description:
Specifies a time limit in seconds for the http requests made. The default value is 35 seconds.
Alternatively, this can be specified using the TWINGATE_HTTP_TIMEOUT environment variable

network.description: Your Twingate network ID for API operations. You can find it in the Admin Console URL, for example: `autoco.twingate.com`, where `autoco` is your network ID Alternatively, this can be specified using the TWINGATE_NETWORK environment variable.

url.description:
The default is 'twingate.com'
This is optional and shouldn't be changed under normal circumstances.

groupsEnabled.description: Specifies whether the provider should cache groups. The default value is `true`.

groupsFilter.description: Specifies the filter for the groups to be cached.

resourceEnabled.description: Specifies whether the provider should cache resources. The default value is `true`.

resourcesFilter.description: Specifies the filter for the resources to be cached.

isActive.description: Returns only Groups matching the specified state.

name.description: Returns only groups that exactly match this name. If no options are passed it will return all resources. Only one option can be used at a time.

nameContains.description: Match when the value exist in the name of the group.

nameExclude.description: Match when the exact value does not exist in the name of the group.

namePrefix.description: The name of the group must start with the value.

nameRegexp.description: The regular expression match of the name of the group.

nameSuffix.description: The name of the group must end with the value.

types.description: Returns groups that match a list of types. valid types: `MANUAL`, `SYNCED`, `SYSTEM`.

name.description: Returns only resources that exactly match this name. If no options are passed it will return all resources. Only one option can be used at a time.

nameContains.description: Match when the value exist in the name of the resource.

nameExclude.description: Match when the exact value does not exist in the name of the resource.

namePrefix.description: The name of the resource must start with the value.

nameRegexp.description: The regular expression match of the name of the resource.

nameSuffix.description: The name of the resource must end with the value.

tags.description: Returns only resources that exactly match the given tags.
A map of key-value pair tags to be set on all resources by default.

A map of key-value pair tags to be set on all resources by default.

domains.description: A set of allowed domains. Defaults to an empty set.

isAuthoritative.description: Whether Terraform should override changes made outside of Terraform. Defaults to true.

blockAdultContent.description: Whether to block adult content. Defaults to false.

blockDating.description: Whether to block dating content. Defaults to false.

blockGambling.description: Whether to block gambling content. Defaults to false.

blockGames.description: Whether to block games. Defaults to false.

blockPiracy.description: Whether to block piracy sites. Defaults to false.

blockSocialMedia.description: Whether to block social media. Defaults to false.

blockStreaming.description: Whether to block streaming content. Defaults to false.

enableSafesearch.description: Whether to force safe search. Defaults to false.

enableYoutubeRestrictedMode.description: Whether to force YouTube to use restricted mode. Defaults to false.

domains.description: A set of denied domains. Defaults to an empty set.

isAuthoritative.description: Whether Terraform should override changes made outside of Terraform. Defaults to true.

blockAdsAndTrackers.description: Whether to block ads and trackers. Defaults to false.

blockAffiliateLinks.description: Whether to block affiliate links. Defaults to false.

blockDisguisedTrackers.description: Whether to block disguised third party trackers. Defaults to false.

blockCryptojacking.description: Whether to block cryptojacking sites. Defaults to true.

blockDnsRebinding.description: Blocks public DNS entries from returning private IP addresses. Defaults to true.

blockDomainGenerationAlgorithms.description: Blocks DGA domains. Defaults to true.

blockIdnHomoglyph.description: Whether to block homoglyph attacks. Defaults to true.

blockNewlyRegisteredDomains.description: Blocks newly registered domains. Defaults to true.

blockParkedDomains.description: Block parked domains. Defaults to true.

blockTyposquatting.description: Blocks typosquatted domains. Defaults to true.

enableGoogleSafeBrowsing.description: Whether to use Google Safe browsing lists to block content. Defaults to true.

enableThreatIntelligenceFeeds.description: Whether to filter content using threat intelligence feeds. Defaults to true.

resources.description: List of Kubernetes resources. Accepts full twingate*kubernetes*resource references.

ca.description: SSH CA configuration. Specify either vault.address or private*key*file, not both.

gateway.description: SSH gateway settings. All fields are optional and fall back to built-in defaults.

resources.description: List of SSH resources. Accepts full twingate*ssh*resource references.

privateKeyFile.description: Path to the SSH CA private key file. Can't be used together with vault.address.

address.description: Vault server address. Can't be used together with ca.private*key*file.

caBundleFile.description: Path to the Vault CA bundle file. Default: "/etc/ssl/vault-ca.crt".

mount.description: Vault SSH secrets engine mount path. Default: "ssh".

role.description: Vault role for signing certificates. Default: "gateway".

gcp.description: GCP authentication for Vault. Can't be used together with token.

token.description: Vault token used for authentication. Can't be used together with gcp.

mount.description: Vault GCP auth mount path. Default: "gcp".

role.description: GCP IAM role for Vault GCP authentication.

serviceAccountEmail.description: Service account email. Required when type is "iam".

type.description: GCP authentication type for Vault (e.g. "iam" or "gce"). When set to "iam", service*account*email is required.

certificateFile.description: Path to the TLS certificate file. Default: "/etc/gateway/tls.crt".

privateKeyFile.description: Path to the TLS private key file. Default: "/etc/gateway/tls.key".

accessPolicies.description: Restrict access according to JIT access policy

groupId.description: Group ID that will have permission to access the Resource.

securityPolicyId.description: The ID of a `twingate.getTwingateSecurityPolicy` to use as the access policy for the group IDs in the access block. Default is 'Null' which points to `Default Policy` on Admin console.

approvalMode.description: This will set the approval model for the policy. The valid values are `AUTOMATIC` and `MANUAL`.

duration.description: This will set the access duration for the policy. Duration must be between 1 hour and 365 days. Examples of valid values include `1h` and `2d`.

mode.description: This will set the accessPolicy mode for the policy. The valid values are `MANUAL`, `AUTO_LOCK` and `ACCESS_REQUEST`.

approvalMode.description: This will set the approval model for the policy. The valid values are `AUTOMATIC` and `MANUAL`.

duration.description: This will set the access duration for the policy. Duration must be between 1 hour and 365 days. Examples of valid values include `1h` and `2d`.

mode.description: This will set the accessPolicy mode for the policy. The valid values are `MANUAL`, `AUTO_LOCK` and `ACCESS_REQUEST`.

policy.description: Whether to allow or deny all ports, or restrict protocol access within certain port ranges: Can be `RESTRICTED` (only listed ports are allowed), `ALLOW_ALL`, or `DENY_ALL`

ports.description: List of port ranges between 1 and 65535 inclusive, in the format `100-200` for a range, or `8080` for a single port

id.description: ID of the Service Account resource

keyIds.description: List of twingate*service*account_key IDs that are assigned to the Service Account.

name.description: Name of the Service Account

resourceIds.description: List of twingate.TwingateResource IDs that the Service Account is assigned to.

email.description: The email address of the User

firstName.description: The first name of the User

id.description: The ID of the User

lastName.description: The last name of the User

role.description: Indicates the User's role. Either ADMIN, DEVOPS, SUPPORT, MEMBER or ACCESS_REVIEWER.

type.description: Indicates the User's type. Either MANUAL or SYNCED.

provider.description:
The provider type for the twingate package. By default, resources use package-wide configuration
settings, however an explicit `Provider` instance may be created and passed during resource
construction to achieve fine-grained programmatic control over provider settings. See the
[documentation](https://www.pulumi.com/docs/reference/programming-model/#providers) for more information.

apiToken.description:
The access key for API operations. You can retrieve this
from the Twingate Admin Console ([documentation](https://docs.twingate.com/docs/api-overview)).
Alternatively, this can be specified using the TWINGATE_API_TOKEN environment variable.

cache.description: Specifies the cache settings for the provider.

defaultTags.description: A default set of tags applied globally to all resources created by the provider.

httpMaxRetry.description:
Specifies a retry limit for the http requests made. The default value is 10.
Alternatively, this can be specified using the TWINGATE_HTTP_MAX_RETRY environment variable

httpTimeout.description:
Specifies a time limit in seconds for the http requests made. The default value is 35 seconds.
Alternatively, this can be specified using the TWINGATE_HTTP_TIMEOUT environment variable

network.description: Your Twingate network ID for API operations. You can find it in the Admin Console URL, for example: `autoco.twingate.com`, where `autoco` is your network ID Alternatively, this can be specified using the TWINGATE_NETWORK environment variable.

url.description:
The default is 'twingate.com'
This is optional and shouldn't be changed under normal circumstances.

The access key for API operations. You can retrieve this
from the Twingate Admin Console ([documentation](https://docs.twingate.com/docs/api-overview)).
Alternatively, this can be specified using the TWINGATE_API_TOKEN environment variable.

statusUpdatesEnabled.description: Determines whether status notifications are enabled for the Connector. Default is `true`.

alias.description: Set a DNS alias address for the Resource. Must be a DNS-valid name string.

gatewayId.description: The ID of the Gateway used to access this Kubernetes Resource.

inCluster.description: Whether the Gateway is running inside the same Kubernetes cluster that is represented by the Kubernetes Resource. Default is `true`.

isVisible.description: Controls whether this Resource will be visible in the main Resource list in the Twingate Client. Default is `true`.

name.description: The name of the Kubernetes Resource.

protocols.description: Restrict access to certain protocols and ports. By default or when this argument is not defined, there is no restriction, and all protocols and ports are allowed.

remoteNetworkId.description: The ID of the Remote Network the Kubernetes Resource belongs to.

securityPolicyId.description: The ID of a `twingate.getTwingateSecurityPolicy` to set as this Resource's Security Policy. Default is 'Null' which points to `Default Policy` on Admin console.

tags.description: A map of key-value pair tags to set on this resource.

location.description: The location of the Remote Network. Must be one of the following: AWS, AZURE, GOOGLE*CLOUD, ON*PREMISE, OTHER.

name.description: The name of the Remote Network

type.description: The type of the Remote Network. Must be one of the following: REGULAR, EXIT. Defaults to REGULAR.

twingate:index/twingateResource:Twingate.description:
Resources in Twingate represent servers on the private network that clients can connect to. Resources can be defined by IP, CIDR range, FQDN, or DNS zone. For more information, see the Twingate [documentation](https://docs.twingate.com/docs/resources-and-access-nodes).

## Import

```sh
$ pulumi import twingate:index/twingateResource:TwingateResource resource UmVzb3VyY2U6MzQwNDQ3
```

accessPolicies.description: Restrict access according to JIT access policy

accessServices.description: Restrict access to certain service account

address.description: The Resource's IP/CIDR or FQDN/DNS zone

alias.description: Set a DNS alias address for the Resource. Must be a DNS-valid name string.

isActive.description: Set the resource as active or inactive. Default is `true`.

isAuthoritative.description: Determines whether assignments in the access block will override any existing assignments. Default is `true`. If set to `false`, assignments made outside of Terraform will be ignored.

isBrowserShortcutEnabled.description: Controls whether an "Open in Browser" shortcut will be shown for this Resource in the Twingate Client. Default is `false`.

isVisible.description: Controls whether this Resource will be visible in the main Resource list in the Twingate Client. Default is `true`.

name.description: The name of the Resource

protocols.description: Restrict access to certain protocols and ports. By default or when this argument is not defined, there is no restriction, and all protocols and ports are allowed.

remoteNetworkId.description: Remote Network ID where the Resource lives

securityPolicyId.description: The ID of a `twingate.getTwingateSecurityPolicy` to set as this Resource's Security Policy. Default is 'Null' which points to `Default Policy` on Admin console.

tags.description: A map of key-value pair tags to set on this resource.

tagsAll.description: A map of key-value pairs that represents all tags on this resource, including default tags from provider configuration.

twingate:index/twingateServiceAccountKey.description: A Service Key authorizes access to all Resources assigned to a Service Account.

expirationTime.description: Specifies how many days until a Service Account Key expires. This should be an integer between 0 and 365 representing the number of days until the Service Account Key will expire. Defaults to 0, meaning the key will never expire.

isActive.description: If the value of this attribute changes to false, Terraform will destroy and recreate the resource.

name.description: The name of the Service Key

serviceAccountId.description: The id of the Service Account

token.description: Autogenerated Service Key token. Used to configure a Twingate Client running in headless mode.

role.description: Determines the User's role. Either ADMIN, DEVOPS, SUPPORT, MEMBER or ACCESS_REVIEWER.

sendInvite.description: Determines whether to send an email invitation to the User. True by default.
sendInvite.deprecationMessage: This attribute is no longer used and will be removed in a future release.

twingate:index/getTwingateRemoteNetwork:.description: A Remote Network represents a single private network in Twingate that can have one or more Connectors and Resources assigned to it. You must create a Remote Network before creating Resources and Connectors that belong to it. For more information, see Twingate's [documentation](https://docs.twingate.com/docs/remote-networks).

lastNameExclude.description: Match when the value does not exist in the last name of the user.

lastNamePrefix.description: The last name of the user must start with the value.

lastNameRegexp.description: The regular expression match of the last name of the user.

lastNameSuffix.description: The last name of the user must end with the value.

roles.description: Returns users that match a list of roles. Valid roles: `ADMIN`, `DEVOPS`, `SUPPORT`, `MEMBER`.

outputs.description: A collection of values returned by getTwingateUsers.

email.description: Returns only users that exactly match this email.

emailContains.description: Match when the value exist in the email of the user.

emailExclude.description: Match when the value does not exist in the email of the user.

emailPrefix.description: The email of the user must start with the value.

emailRegexp.description: The regular expression match of the email of the user.

emailSuffix.description: The email of the user must end with the value.

firstName.description: Returns only users that exactly match the first name.

firstNameContains.description: Match when the value exist in the first name of the user.

firstNameExclude.description: Match when the value does not exist in the first name of the user.

firstNamePrefix.description: The first name of the user must start with the value.

firstNameRegexp.description: The regular expression match of the first name of the user.

firstNameSuffix.description: The first name of the user must end with the value.

id.description: The ID of this resource.
